Title: Phytophthora sojae Avirulence Effector Avr3b is a Secreted NADH and ADP-ribose Pyrophosphorylase that Modulates Plant Immunity

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: P6497 avirulence allele; P7076 virulence allele
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: N. benthamiana NA; soybean Williams, L88-1479, PRX146-36, L77-1863
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: NADH/NAD+ turnover contributes to redox homeostasis and ROS balance; ADP-ribose recycling is linked to stress and immunity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: intracellular plant cells at infection sites
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Nudix hydrolase-mediated nucleotide metabolite turnover / ADP-ribose and NADH catabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ADP-ribose/NADH pyrophosphorylase branch of Nudix hydrolase metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: RXLR effector; Nudix hydrolase motif; W motif
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: secreted effector; functions inside host cells
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: strongly induced in cysts, germinating cysts, and infected tissue; ~20-fold in cysts, ~150-fold in germinating cysts, ~50-fold at 24 hpi versus mycelia; lower transcript accumulation in P7076 than P6497
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phytophthora infection; transient expression in N. benthamiana followed by P. capsici or P. parasitica challenge
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: AMP + ribose-5-phosphate from ADP-ribose; AMP + reduced nicotinamide mononucleotide (NMNH) from NADH
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes ADP-ribose and NADH pyrophosphate bonds, acting as an ADP-ribose/NADH pyrophosphorylase
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: catabolic turnover step in nucleotide-derivative metabolism
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly degrades target metabolites and modulates host redox/nucleotide pools
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes Phytophthora virulence, suppresses ROS accumulation and ETI, and elevates ADP-ribose/NADH pyrophosphorylase activity in planta; avirulence allele triggers Rps3b-dependent cell death
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Avh307 was identified as Avr3b by cosegregation, polymorphism analysis, and soybean transient expression. 2. Avr3b encodes a secreted RXLR effector with a C-terminal Nudix motif and has ADP-ribose/NADH pyrophosphorylase activity. 3. Mutating conserved Nudix residues abolished most enzyme activity and impaired virulence functions, including suppression of ROS and ETI and enhancement of pathogen biomass. 4. Silencing Avr3b in P. sojae reduced virulence on susceptible soybean. 5. The C-terminal region is sufficient for Rps3b-dependent recognition, but an intact Nudix motif is not strictly required for avirulence activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: genetic cosegregation mapping; sequence polymorphism analysis; transient expression in soybean and N. benthamiana; western blot; immunoprecipitation; in vitro enzyme assay; total extract enzyme assay; dsRNA silencing in P. sojae; pathogen biomass quantification; ROS staining; ETI suppression assay
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: digital gene expression (Solexa DGE) [35]; Affymetrix microarray data [12,31]; EST mining; resequencing/454 genome sequencing of P. sojae lineages P7064, P7074, P7076
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter (soybean bombardment constructs); PVX vector pGR107 for transient expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for understanding pathogen effector-mediated metabolic suppression of immunity and for deployment of corresponding resistance genes such as Rps3b; Nudix catalytic residues are candidate targets for anti-virulence strategies, though this is a pathogen gene rather than a crop quality improvement target
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Virulence allele retains enzyme activity while escaping Rps3b recognition; effector manipulation is pathogen-focused rather than beneficial nutrient biofortification
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The virulence allele Avr3bP7076 is truncated and highly polymorphic but still retains pyrophosphorylase activity and virulence function. The authors note that because Avr3b was not purified to homogeneity, indirect co-purification of a plant Nudix hydrolase cannot be absolutely excluded.
